title: salix × sendaica subsp. ultima
intro: Salix × sendaica subsp. ultima, also known as the Japanese willow or Sakaki, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the Salix genus, characterized by its small leaves and slender branches. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix × sendaica subsp. ultima include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In traditional Japanese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or powder and is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems.
